headline_summary: US–Israel strikes on Iran continued with expanded target sets and reports of intensified attacks in Tehran
Strait of Hormuz shipping disruption risk remained elevated, with tanker availability and war-risk insurance premia rising
Major carriers and shipowners adjusted routes/bookings amid security concerns, tightening global freight capacity
Cyber/information operations increased alongside kinetic operations, raising miscalculation and escalation risk
Markets remained focused on oil supply and shipping chokepoint risk translating into broader risk-off conditions
risk_drivers: Escalatory strike tempo and widening target sets
Persistent Strait of Hormuz disruption/insurance risk
Potential spillover to regional states and bases
Retaliation threats against international shipping and overseas assets

Energy: Upside skew for crude and refined products on Hormuz chokepoint and tanker insurance constraints (oil_up_risk)
Higher freight/war-risk premia for tankers and LNG; supply chain delays feed energy-price volatility
FX: Risk-off supports USD and safe-haven FX; pressure on EM/high-beta FX (em_fx_pressure, risk_off_flows)
Rates: Flight-to-quality can compress core yields while oil-shock inflation risk and uncertainty widen credit spreads (rates_credit_spreads)
Equities: Higher energy and freight costs weigh on cyclicals/transport; relative support for energy and defense

sources.title: Bombing of Tehran intensifies as war enters day six
US says Iran firing fewer missiles, US strikes to expand inland
Strait of Hormuz crisis: shipping, trade and oil markets
